headline: NASAO: 467 airframes grounded across 25 states + Wisconsin: 100% of state drone fleet grounded — procurement officers sourcing Blue UAS thermal sensors face the highest near-term supply risk since 2024.

fcc_countdown.message: 268 days until FCC Blue UAS exemption expires (Jan 1, 2027). Agencies running DJI/Autel under exemption must complete platform transition or secure individual exemption.

lead_story.title: NASAO: 467 airframes grounded across 25 states — $50M–$2B national exposure
lead_story.body: Oregon/NASAO Revision 2 white paper (Feb 2026): 467 drone airframes grounded or restricted across 23 states per survey of 25 state DOTs. National replacement exposure: $50M–$2B. Idaho benchmark: $15K DJI → $42K compliant (2.8x premium). Triggered by OMB M-26-02 (Nov 21 2025), FHWA guidance, and FCC Covered List action (Dec 22 2025). Phase-out deadline Dec 2027. NASAO recommends congressional waiver through Sept 2027.
lead_story.action: Oregon Dept of Aviation / NASAO multi-state survey. 25 state DOTs. 467 airframes grounded. Revised Feb 2026.

sources.name: Oregon/NASAO White Paper

gray_zone.entity: [GRAY ZONE] Anzu Robotics: Teardown confirms adversary hardware: PCB layouts align exactly with DJI Mavic 3
gray_zone.development: Teardown confirms adversary hardware: PCB layouts align exactly with DJI Mavic 3 Enterprise. Board part numbers match. Controller RRC01 is relabeled DJI RC Pro with identical firmware. Camera module, gimbal, propulsion all DJI-sourced. DJI P1 OcuSync chipset present — proprietary ASIC that cannot be independently sourced.

gray_zone.entity: [GRAY ZONE] Anzu Robotics: Technology licensed from adversary entity: DJI (Shenzhen DJI Technology Co., Ltd
gray_zone.development: Technology licensed from adversary entity: DJI (Shenzhen DJI Technology Co., Ltd.)

gray_zone.entity: [GRAY ZONE] Anzu Robotics: Firmware signed with adversary crypto keys: Firmware signed and encrypted with D
gray_zone.development: Firmware signed with adversary crypto keys: Firmware signed and encrypted with DJI cryptographic keys — DJI retains root key control. Built on DJI SDK. SecNeo/BangCle Chinese security framework present — not confirmed decrypted. MAVProxyUser documented firmware accessible on DJI's own CDN, contradicting independent hosting claims.

supply_chain.signal: GUR War & Sanctions portal and Ukrainian military intelligence confirmed Raspberry Pi 4B as the core of the 'Borscht Tracker V3' in Geran-2 (Shahed-136) and Geran-3 (Shahed-238) attack drones. Reports indicate 40,000+ units procured via gray market channels. RPi CEO Eben Upton acknowledged use in Russian drones and tightened end-use requirements for China sales. Cross-reference: RPi CM4 used by Freefly Astro (Blue UAS) and custom ArduPilot builds.
supply_chain.window: RPi gray market premium in China remains elevated (30-50% above MSRP). Active pr

supply_chain.signal: GUR documented RPi 5 integrated alongside Chinese Mini PC F8 (rebranded as 'Raskat') running Windows 11 in the Molniya-2R reconnaissance UAV. The Molniya-2R is the recon variant of the kamikaze FPV drone, used for monitoring Ukrainian positions and strike adjustment. Confirms active, ongoing procurement of latest-gen RPi hardware despite tightened export controls.

supply_chain.signal: Russia's Geran-5 (6m wingspan, jet-powered, 90kg warhead, 1000km range) uses a 12-channel Kometa satellite navigation system and a tracker based on RPi with 3G/4G modems. The Geran-5 also features XK-F358 mesh network modems enabling 'chain control' — multiple drones in a salvo maintaining comms. This represents escalation from simple GPS-guided to mesh-networked swarm capability, all built on commercially available SBCs.

watch_list.item: Axon + Skydio: body cam → drone dispatch → CJIS evidence chain (highest lock-in)
watch_list.why: Axon (18,000+ LE agencies) partnered with Skydio (June 2024). Integrated DFR → body camera → Axon Evidence CJIS-complian
watch_list.trigger: Axon-Skydio dominates LE DFR by end 2026. CJIS-compliant drone evidence will become municipal attorn

watch_list.item: Ohio statewide DFR pilot (SkyfireAI/ODOT): first participants identified, $110-230K per site
watch_list.why: Ohio identified first municipal participants in nation's only statewide DFR pilot. SkyfireAI managing under ODOT/DriveOh
watch_list.trigger: Ohio pilot will produce publishable outcomes by Q4 2026 that trigger 3-5 additional states to launch

watch_list.item: Policy: FCC blocks new foreign drone imports (equipment authorization freeze)
watch_list.why: Date: 2025-12. Domestic Blue UAS demand spike — no new Chinese drone models can enter market Affected manufacturers: All
watch_list.trigger: Demand shift toward domestic/allied components. Monitor lead times for Jetson Orin NX, QRB5165.

predictions.event: 74% of pending COW applications stalled — incomplete submissions, 7 common errors documented
